mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it ideal for applications requiring reliable and scalable storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to simpler storage solutions.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Rook using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-ceph' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rook-ceph

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

```

> Creates a namespace for Rook.
> Installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create the Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster by applying the Rook cluster custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/cluster.yaml

```

> Deploys the Ceph cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify the Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

```

> Lists all pods in the rook-ceph namespace to verify they are running.
> Executes a command in the Rook tools pod to check the Ceph cluster status.
commands: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Ceph cluster and all data stored within it.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph

kubectl delete namespace rook-ceph

```

> Uninstalls the Rook operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step upgrades the Rook operator and verifies the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-ceph drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

```

> Drains the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the Rook operator.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create pool'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Ceph cluster is not properly initialized or if there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Ceph cluster is running and check resource allocation.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are configuration errors in the Ceph cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph status shows 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are issues with the Ceph cluster that need to be addressed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph health detail' to get more information.

troubleshooting.title: Failed to connect to Ceph cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network issues or misconfigured endpoints can cause this error.

**Fix:**
Verify network connectivity and check the Ceph configuration for correct endpoints.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new storage classes and improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration options for storage pools.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.18.x, review the new storage class configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod' showing all pods in the 'Running' state, and 'ceph status' should report 'HEALTH_OK'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
